setting.note: The summer of 1922 on the North Shore of Long Island and in Manhattan. West Egg and East Egg are Fitzgerald's names for the real Great Neck (Kings Point) and Sands Point peninsulas, facing each other across Manhasset Bay. The valley of ashes is the Corona ash dumps in Queens, passed on the road and railway into the city under the eyes of Doctor T. J. Eckleburg. The Plaza is the Plaza.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. F. Scott Fitzgerald, first published 1925; the author died in 1940.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1925, entered the US public domain in 2021), and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1940).
rights.attribution: F. Scott Fitzgerald, The Great Gatsby (1925). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The green light at the end of Daisy's dock, the one thing Gatsby watches across the bay. A colour drawn straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
